text:
The Auctioneer is a female auctioneer who works at the Southernpiece Auction House. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Appearance

The Auctioneer wears a black dress and a green brooch around her neck. At the same time, she has purple hair and orange eyes in the 2011 anime adaptation, and orange hair and purple eyes in the 1999 anime adaptation, and her hair is tied back into three circular buns at the back of her head.

Background

She is an auctioneer at the Southernpiece Auction who auctioned off the Greed Island games. 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Addressing all auction attendees, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 120 the Auctioneer welcomes everyone and notes that every item up for auction has special characteristics. She also flatters the audience with praise, noting that they're special for being true and earnest collectors. With the introductions over, the Auctioneer formally starts the auction by presenting a Fossilized Dropping from a Deodorosaurus. 

Afterward, the Auctioneer finally presents the mysterious game, Greed Island. She directed the audience to the screen, where a JoyStation Console is presented. The Auctioneer confirms that this JoyStation Console does not run on normal electricity, but on a "Mysterious Energy". 

A muscular fellow wielding a sledgehammer walks on stage to demonstrate the durability of the Greed Island game in a JoyStation Console with a player already inside. He tries to smash it to bits with his hammer, but fails. The Auctioneer explains that the "Mysterious Energy" protects against external attacks as long as a player is inside the game. The Auctioneer then introduces the player inside the game called Jeitsari, who owns seven copies of Greed Island, the combined value of which equals a total of 5.8 billion . 

The Auctioneer further explains how the auction house procured several copies of Greed Island under Jeitsari's contract, then details what happens when a player dies in the game. With the explanations completed, the Auctioneer begins bidding at 1 billion . A fervent bid war takes place between attendees #71 & #16. During it, attendee #201 accidentally doubles the amount, but in the end, #16 wins the copy of the game. 

By the end of the auction, the Auctioneer auctioned off two more copies of the game; one for 27.8 billion and the other for 60.2 billion , both copies won by the same person. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 124 

Trivia

In the 1999 anime adaptation, Auctioneer is credited as Facilitator in the credits.
